title: Sunday, November 12
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Sermon: "Sunday, November 12" | Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver
Preacher: Broderick Greer | Date: November 12, 2017

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This date falls on the Twenty-Third Sunday after Pentecost in the Church Year (Ordinary Time), just three weeks before the First Sunday of Advent. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this Sunday were likely 1 Thessalonians 5:1-11, Matthew 25:14-30 (the Parable of the Talents), Psalm 90, and Judges 4:12-7. This particular Sunday emphasizes themes of watchfulness, stewardship, and readiness for Christ's return, fitting the eschatological mood of the liturgical year's conclusion.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church remained in the aftermath of the June 2018 General Convention being several months away, with ongoing debates about sexuality, immigration, and the Trump administration's policies within Anglican circles. Bishop Robert Wright had been leading the Diocese of Colorado since 2013 and would have been active in diocesan affairs. There were no major Episcopal leadership crises or elections immediately surrounding this date, though the broader church continued processing its institutional identity in the Trump era.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver experienced typical November weather patterns with potential early snow in the mountains; the city itself was entering the holiday retail season. The Colorado Springs church shooting tragedy of October 25 (at the New Life Church) would have been fresh in regional consciousness, affecting interfaith dialogue and church security discussions across Colorado denominations. The downtown Denver area and Capitol Hill were managing typical mid-November activity with holiday preparations underway.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Roy Moore sexual misconduct allegations dominated headlines after the Washington Post published its investigation on November 9, creating a significant political crisis for the Alabama Senate race. The Trump administration was in its tenth month, with ongoing investigations into Russian interference and associated legal proceedings. The nation remained polarized over healthcare (the failed Republican tax bill efforts), immigration, and social justice movements continuing from the previous year.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Saudi Arabian purge continued following Prince Salman's anti-corruption campaign that began in early November, with prominent arrests and detention of princes and business figures. The Iraqi Army was completing major operations against ISIS remnants in Iraq after the declaration of military victory over ISIS territorial control. Myanmar's Rohingya crisis was well underway, with hundreds of thousands of refugees fleeing to Bangladesh following military operations beginning in late August.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was in a transitional moment between the post-election 2016 reckoning and an increasingly fractious national discourse characterized by #MeToo momentum (gaining force in the Roy Moore context). The holiday season was beginning with retail and entertainment industries in full operation. Sports culture was marked by ongoing NFL national anthem protests and debates, while the Denver Broncos (3-6 record) were struggling through a disappointing season, a departure from their Super Bowl glory of recent years.
